author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: Prayer and Exhortation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: There are some, moreover, who either do not pray at all, or pray coldly, because, from the Lord’s words, they have learnt that God knows what is necessary for us before we ask it of Him.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Must the truth of this declaration be given up, or shall we think that it should be erased from the gospel because of such people?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Nay, since it is manifest that God has prepared some things to be given even to those who do not pray for them, such as the beginning of faith, and other things not to be given except to those who pray for them, such as perseverance even unto the end, certainly he who thinks that he has this latter from himself does not pray to have it.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore we must take care lest, while we are afraid of exhortation growing lukewarm, prayer should be stifled and arrogance stimulated.
